title: nasturtium hirtum
intro: Nasturtium hirtum, also known as Indian cress or sticky cress, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The plant's leaves and flowers have been consumed as a salad green or used in herbal preparations to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, nasturtium hirtum is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
